trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at score 60.1 (1.080ms vs 1.090ms reference). Profiling shows GEMM at 85.6%, GroupNorm at 10.3%, with post-GEMM ops totaling ~14%. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: You've spent significant time debugging a custom Triton GroupNorm kernel. Consider a simpler approach: keep PyTorch's `nn.GroupNorm` (which is already optimized) and only write a Triton kernel to fuse the min-reduction + bias-add step, or try `torch.compile` on the full forward pass first. The GEMM and GroupNorm are already well-optimized in PyTorch — the main optimization opportunity is fusing the post-GroupNorm operations.

The score of 60.1 is minimal improvement. Try `torch.compile(mode="default")` on the forward method — it can auto-fuse GroupNorm+Min+Bias and potentially save memory round-trips. Also consider that fusing GroupNorm+Min into one Triton kernel (reading the GEMM output once, computing per-group stats and min simultaneously) would eliminate the intermediate 32MB GroupNorm output tensor, which could meaningfully help since GroupNorm is 10% of runtime.
